title: Backtracking search

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Backtracking search; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Backtracking search
sources.claim: Backtracking explores a decision tree, abandons partial states that cannot lead to a solution, and restores state before trying alternatives.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Backtracking explores a decision tree, abandons partial states that cannot lead to a solution, and restores state before trying alternatives.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A Sudoku solver places a candidate, recurses, and clears that cell when the branch fails. Your goal is to implement and test a permutation, subset or constraint-placement solver with pruning. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Recursion and divide-and-conquer; Hash tables, sets and maps; Recursive problem design; Time, space and asymptotic growth. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Backtracking explores a decision tree, abandons partial states that cannot lead to a solution, and restores state before trying alternatives. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Worst-case search remains exponential, pruning must be sound, and shared mutable state must be undone exactly. The draft therefore rejects: Backtracking becomes polynomial whenever one pruning condition is added.

includes: Choose, recurse, undo and prune a decision tree while preserving candidate state.
Implement and test a permutation, subset or constraint-placement solver with pruning.

Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.
excludes: Worst-case search remains exponential, pruning must be sound, and shared mutable state must be undone exactly.

outcomes: Implement and test a permutation, subset or constraint-placement solver with pruning.

examples.title: Backtracking search: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: A Sudoku solver places a candidate, recurses, and clears that cell when the branch fails.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: A Sudoku solver places a candidate, recurses, and clears that cell when the branch fails.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Pruning can reduce explored states but does not automatically change exponential worst-case complexity.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Pruning can reduce explored states but does not automatically change exponential worst-case complexity.

examples.title: Backtracking search: implementation transfer
examples.situation: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Worst-case search remains exponential, pruning must be sound, and shared mutable state must be undone exactly.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Backtracking becomes polynomial whenever one pruning condition is added.
misconceptions.correction: Pruning can reduce explored states but does not automatically change exponential worst-case complexity.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use an invariant or counterexample to explain why this DSA belief fails: Backtracking becomes polynomial whenever one pruning condition is added.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Backtracking search, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.
usefulFor: Introducing structure, order, connectivity, or state transition before formal notation
limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
Worst-case search remains exponential, pruning must be sound, and shared mutable state must be undone exactly.
